figures.caption: Table 1. Performance comparison with preference learning based hallucination mitigation methods for MLLMs across various hallucination benchmarks. For baselines without official checkpoints, the results are taken from the respective papers. For baselines with available official checkpoints, we primarily refer to the re-evaluation results reported in [37]. The † indicates that the method was trained on the same dataset as ours. The best for each metric is in bold.

Table 2. Ablation study of exploration control on the preferred and dispreferred branches with LLaVA-v1.5-7B. The w/o pref. indicates that exploration control is retained only on the dispref. branch, while the pref. branch reverts to the standard DPO logprobability summation. Conversely, the w/o dispref. applies control only to the pref. branch. The complete UE-DPO method applies exploration control to both branches.

Figure 3. Visualization of the token-wise visual sensitivity and epistemic uncertainty in generating the corresponding responses. Positive values are indicated by red blocks and negative values by blue blocks, with deeper colors indicating larger absolute magnitudes at the corresponding positions. White denotes positions with a value of zero. The examples are ground-truth preferred samples from RLHF-V.
